messages.subject: [R-meta] Use of Hedges 1992 publication bias correction method
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta] Use of Hedges 1992 publication bias correction method
messages.body_plain:
Dear Matthias and Michael,

Thank you so much for the information! I was able to install and use the
weightr package.

Best regards,
Chishio

On Thu, Sep 27, 2018 at 8:22 PM Michael Dewey <lists at dewey.myzen.co.uk>
wrote:

> Dear Chisio
>
> There are a number of alternative methods mentioned in the CRAN Task
> View on MetaAnalysis https://cran.r-project.org/view=MetaAnalysis. Look
> for the section headed "Unobserved studies".
>
> Michael (maintainer of the task view)
>
>
> On 27/09/2018 05:38, Chishio Furukawa wrote:
> > Dear all,
> >
> > I am writing to ask if anyone knows a statistical package that provides
> > Hedges (1992) method to correct publication bias. It assumes that
> > publication probability is a step function of p-value, and provides
> > estimates using the maximum likelihood with assumptions that the
> underlying
> > distributions are normal. It is commonly used in economics, but I could
> not
> > find packages that automate this approach.
> >
> > Please let me know if anyone knows about this!
> >
> > Best regards,
> > Chishio
> >
>
> --
> Michael
> http://www.dewey.myzen.co.uk/home.html
>


-- 
Chishio Furukawa
?????
Phone: (090)-6487-1382

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
